channel.title: Saurabh Jain
channel.description: Security engineer writing about cloud security, bug bounties, AWS, and the grey areas no one puts on a slide deck.

item.title: BountyHunter Walkthrough: HackTheBox Writeup
item.description: BountyHunter is a beginner Linux machine on HackTheBox. This walkthrough covers nmap recon, exploiting an XXE vulnerability in a bug report form to read local files, and privilege escalation via a Python sudo script.

item.title: TomGhost Write-Up: TryHackMe Walk-through
item.description: A walkthrough of the TomGhost easy Linux machine on TryHackMe, exploiting Apache Tomcat via the Apache JServ Protocol (AJP) Ghostcat vulnerability (CVE-2020-1938).

item.title: Cap Walk-through: HackTheBox Machine Write-up
item.description: Cap is a beginner Linux machine on HackTheBox. This walkthrough covers nmap recon, discovering a PCAP download vulnerability exposing FTP credentials, and privilege escalation via Linux capabilities.

item.title: BackDoor 1: NET-SQUARE Hacking Warm-Up Mobile Application Challenge
item.description: Walkthrough of the BackDoor 1 mobile CTF challenge from NET-SQUARE — extracting hidden credentials from an Android APK using static reverse engineering tools.

item.title: BackDoor 2: NET-SQUARE Hacking Warm-Up Mobile Application Challenge
item.description: Walkthrough of BackDoor 2 from NET-SQUARE's CTF series — credentials are encoded rather than plaintext, requiring a deeper static analysis to locate and decode them.

item.title: BackDoor 3: NET-SQUARE Hacking Warm-Up Mobile Application Challenge
item.description: Walkthrough of BackDoor 3 — the most complex challenge in NET-SQUARE's series, requiring multi-step extraction combining static analysis, runtime instrumentation, and potentially native library inspection.

item.title: Morty Sherlocked: Android Application CTF Challenge Walkthrough
item.description: A beginner-level Android CTF challenge walkthrough covering APK analysis, reverse engineering, and common mobile security vulnerabilities.

item.title: Morty's New Tool: Android Application CTF Challenge Walkthrough
item.description: An intermediate Android CTF challenge involving dynamic code transformation at runtime, native library reverse engineering, and Frida instrumentation to intercept a hidden flag.

item.title: Planet X: Android Application CTF Challenge Walkthrough
item.description: Walkthrough of the Planet X Android CTF challenge — a beginner-friendly challenge teaching foundational mobile application security assessment techniques.

item.title: Universe Weird C132: Android Application CTF Challenge Walkthrough
item.description: Walkthrough of the Universe Weird C132 Android CTF challenge, covering reverse engineering techniques and common mobile application security flaws.
